title: Redirection & Pipes
content:
### Standard Streams

| Name | Descriptor | Symbol |
|------|-----------|--------|
| stdin | 0 | Input to command |
| stdout | 1 | Normal output |
| stderr | 2 | Error output |

### Redirecting Output

```bash
command > file.txt         # redirect stdout to file (overwrite)
command >> file.txt        # redirect stdout to file (append)
command 2> error.log       # redirect stderr to file
command 2>&1               # redirect stderr to stdout
command &> file.txt        # redirect both stdout and stderr
command > /dev/null        # discard output
```

### Redirecting Input

```bash
command < file.txt         # read stdin from file
sort < unsorted.txt > sorted.txt
```

### Pipes — Chain Commands

The pipe `|` sends stdout of one command to stdin of the next.

```bash
ls -la | less
dmesg | grep error
cat access.log | cut -d' ' -f1 | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
```

### tee — Split Output

Sends output to both file and stdout.

```bash
ls -la | tee listing.txt
ls -la | tee -a log.txt    # append mode
```

### xargs — Build and Execute Commands

```bash
find . -name "*.tmp" | xargs rm
cat urls.txt | xargs -n 1 curl -O
find . -type f -name "*.jpg" | xargs -P 4 -I {} convert {} {}.png
```

### /dev/null — The Bit Bucket

```bash
command > /dev/null 2>&1   # completely silence output
```
codeExample:
echo "hello world" > file.txt
cat file.txt | wc -w
ls /nonexistent 2> error.log
ls -la | tee output.txt | wc -l
find . -name "*.log" | xargs rm

quiz.question: What does `command &> file.txt` do?
options: Redirects both stdout and stderr to file
Redirects stdout to file and stderr to terminal
Runs the command in the background

quiz.question: What does `tee` do in a pipeline?
options: Sends output to both a file and stdout
